notes: Indiana, South Dakota, and Wisconsin addenda may exist in Exhibit D-1 (state addenda to FA) but were not found in Exhibit J-1 (state addenda to FDD)

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clauses may be unenforceable under California Civil Code Section 1671
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor's liquidated damages formula (up to 60x avg monthly royalty) may not be collectible in California

overrides.override_summary: California BPC §§20000-20043 provide rights concerning termination and non-renewal that may supersede FA provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees may have stronger protections against termination than the FA provides

overrides.override_summary: New York governing law provision may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California law may apply instead of New York law for California franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Virginia venue provision may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees may be able to litigate in California rather than Virginia

overrides.override_summary: General release of claims at renewal/transfer may be voided by Cal Corp Code §31512 and BPC §20010
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees may not be required to waive claims under California franchise law

overrides.override_summary: General release shall not relieve franchisor from liability imposed by Hawaii franchise law
overrides.why_it_matters: Hawaii franchisees retain all claims under Hawaii law regardless of release language

overrides.override_summary: Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act 815 ILCS 705/19 and 705/20 may affect termination and non-renewal rights
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois franchisees may have additional protections against termination

overrides.override_summary: Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act governs with respect to Illinois licensees; venue/choice of law/jury waiver provisions do not waive rights under Illinois law
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois law may override the FA's New York choice-of-law and Virginia venue provisions

overrides.override_summary: Section 41 of Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act: any condition purporting to waive compliance with the Act is void
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois franchisees cannot be required to waive Illinois franchise law protections

overrides.override_summary: Maryland law may supersede FA provisions on termination and renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees may have stronger termination/renewal protections

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may sue in Maryland for claims arising under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law; 3-year statute of limitations
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees can litigate in Maryland rather than Virginia

overrides.override_summary: General release shall not operate to release franchisor from liability under Maryland law
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees retain Maryland franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: No statement, questionnaire, or acknowledgment shall waive claims under applicable state franchise law or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees cannot be forced to disclaim reliance on FDD representations

overrides.override_summary: Franchise may not be terminated prior to expiration except for good cause; 30-day cure opportunity required
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan's 'good cause' requirement may narrow the 16 non-curable default grounds

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor may not refuse to renew without fair compensation (repurchase at FMV) if term <5 years or franchisee cannot continue same business
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan may require compensation on non-renewal if franchisee cannot continue operating

overrides.override_summary: Provision requiring arbitration or litigation outside Michigan is void
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan franchisees can litigate/arbitrate in Michigan rather than Virginia

overrides.override_summary: Requirement that franchisee assent to release, waiver, or estoppel depriving franchisee of statutory rights is void
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan franchisees cannot be forced to waive Michigan franchise law rights

overrides.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination (60 days to cure) and 180 days notice for non-renewal required under Minn. Stat. §80C.14
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota provides significantly longer cure and notice periods than the FA's 10/30-day periods

overrides.override_summary: Minnesota Rule 2860.4400J prohibits requiring franchisee to consent to liquidated damages
overrides.why_it_matters: Liquidated damages provisions may be unenforceable against Minnesota franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor may not require litigation outside Minnesota, jury trial waiver, or consent to liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota franchisees retain right to litigate in Minnesota with jury trial

overrides.override_summary: General release shall not relieve franchisor from liability under Minnesota Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota franchisees retain all Minnesota franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: New York Franchise Law requires NY law govern causes of action under NY Franchises Law; NY GBL Art. 33 §§680-695 may supersede inconsistent FA provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: New York franchise law governs NY franchisees regardless of FA choice-of-law

overrides.override_summary: General release required at renewal/transfer may not be enforceable under New York law
overrides.why_it_matters: New York franchisees may not be bound by release language

overrides.override_summary: All liquidated damages provisions deleted in their entirety
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota franchisees are NOT subject to any liquidated damages

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota law supersedes New York law if in conflict; FA governed by North Dakota law
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota law fully overrides the NY choice-of-law

overrides.override_summary: Any provision designating venue outside North Dakota is deleted
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota franchisees litigate in North Dakota

overrides.override_summary: Jury trial waiver provision is deleted
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota franchisees retain right to jury trial

overrides.override_summary: Release language shall not relieve franchisor from liability under North Dakota Franchise Disclosure Act
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota franchisees retain all statutory claims

overrides.override_summary: Release signed as condition of transfer or renewal does not apply to claims under Rhode Island Franchise Investment Act
overrides.why_it_matters: Rhode Island franchisees retain statutory claims at renewal/transfer

overrides.override_summary: Provisions restricting jurisdiction/venue outside Rhode Island or requiring non-RI law are void as to claims under RI Franchise Investment Act
overrides.why_it_matters: Rhode Island franchisees can litigate in Rhode Island under RI law

overrides.override_summary: Virginia Retail Franchising Act §13.1-564: unlawful to cancel franchise without reasonable cause
overrides.why_it_matters: Virginia 'reasonable cause' standard may limit some termination grounds

overrides.override_summary: RCW 19.100.180 may supersede FA termination and renewal provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington law may provide additional termination/renewal protections

overrides.override_summary: Release/waiver of rights excludes rights under Washington Franchise Investment Protection Act unless executed in negotiated settlement with independent counsel
overrides.why_it_matters: Standard general releases are ineffective as to Washington franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in Washington or mutually agreed location; franchisee may bring action in Washington for franchise law violations
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington franchisees can litigate in Washington

overrides.override_summary: Noncompetition covenants void unless employee earns >$100K/year or independent contractor earns >$250K/year (adjusted for inflation). No-poach restrictions between franchisees prohibited.
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington significantly limits enforceable noncompete scope and prohibits inter-franchisee no-poach

overrides.override_summary: Washington Franchise Investment Protection Act prevails in conflict of laws
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington law overrides conflicting FA provisions
